text.title: Extreme Programming Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Extreme Programming. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team adopts TDD; CI takes 30 minutes; pair switches context 5 times per cycle.
antipattern.fix: Optimise CI to <10min before TDD; parallel jobs + test sharding + cache warm-up.

antipattern.symptom: Backlog of 'refactor tickets'; each takes 3 days; tests get skipped during the refactor.
antipattern.root-cause: Refactor not done while green in the same session.
antipattern.fix: Add refactor commit immediately after each green; PR template enforces it.

antipattern.symptom: Feature branch lives 3 weeks; merge conflict on every file; CI red on rebase.
antipattern.fix: Switch to short-lived branches; rebase daily; ship behind feature flags.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer guesses at requirement; ships wrong feature; rebuild cost = 1 sprint.
antipattern.fix: Designate a product person with <24h response SLA; route blocking questions through them.
